channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: US Man Cuts His Genitals, Sets Them On Fire To Commit Garage Arson

media:content.media:description: Police charged a Fort Wayne man with felony after self-mutilation and garage fire incident.

item.description: Christopher Peden allegedly mutilated himself and committed arson at a detached garage in Fort Wayne, Indiana.
item.content:encoded: Christopher Peden allegedly mutilated himself and committed arson at a detached garage in Fort Wayne, Indiana.

item.title: 38 Demands, 48-Hour Ultimatum: PoK Is On Brink Of Another Showdown

media:content.media:description: Formed in 2023, the JAAC has rapidly emerged as the most influential civil rights platform in PoJK.

item.description: At the centre of the protests is the demand to abolish 12 legislative assembly seats reserved for Kashmiri refugees settled in Pakistan.
item.content:encoded: At the centre of the protests is the demand to abolish 12 legislative assembly seats reserved for Kashmiri refugees settled in Pakistan.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | Thanks To Trump, Saving The US-Iran Islamabad Deal Is Now Harder Than Ever
item.description: Neither side wants more war. But neither side seems able to stop itself from marching towards one.
item.content:encoded: Neither side wants more war. But neither side seems able to stop itself from marching towards one.

item.title: US Parents File Lawsuit After Daycare Worker Tosses Toddler In Air, Drops Him

media:content.media:description: The incident allegedly took place on March 17, 2025, at the club's on-site childcare facility.

item.description: The suit alleges the child was lifted about six feet above the ground before the employee let go of his hands and failed to catch him. The toddler allegedly fell head-first onto a hardwood floor.
item.content:encoded: The suit alleges the child was lifted about six feet above the ground before the employee let go of his hands and failed to catch him. The toddler allegedly fell head-first onto a hardwood floor.

item.title: "Milk At Home Is Australian, Tea Indian": PM Modi's Top Quotes In Melbourne

media:content.media:description: PM Modi addressed Indian community at an event in Melbourne.

item.description: PM Modi concluded a high-level summit with Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese earlier today, marking an expansion of the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership (CSP).
item.content:encoded: PM Modi concluded a high-level summit with Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese earlier today, marking an expansion of the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership (CSP).

item.title: New Envoys, Old Tensions: Bangladesh-India Ties Enter Fresh Phase

media:content.media:description: Bangladesh is set to name new High Commissioner to India

item.description: As India and Bangladesh navigate a transitional phase in their relationship, Siam's appointment and posting in India will be significant.
item.content:encoded: As India and Bangladesh navigate a transitional phase in their relationship, Siam's appointment and posting in India will be significant.

item.title: Trump Wants To Leave The Iran War Behind. It Won't Be Easy

media:content.media:description: The White House did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

item.description: However, backing down in the face of Iranian defiance could add to Tehran's sense that it can assert leverage over the world's most important oil-shipping channel, anytime it sees fit.
item.content:encoded: However, backing down in the face of Iranian defiance could add to Tehran's sense that it can assert leverage over the world's most important oil-shipping channel, anytime it sees fit.

item.title: How Australian Uranium Can Fuel India's Clean Energy Plans

media:content.media:description: India and Australia have grown considerably closer in recent years

item.description: Australia has around 28 per cent of the world's uranium resources, but it doesn't use any nuclear power or weapons and exports all of it.
item.content:encoded: Australia has around 28 per cent of the world's uranium resources, but it doesn't use any nuclear power or weapons and exports all of it.

item.title: Top Fashion Brands Have A Supply Chain Battle Against Extreme Heat

media:content.media:description: The air conditioning system uses oversized pipes to reduce friction and chiller power consumption.

item.description: Extreme heat is hitting garment workers across Asia hard. See how Epic Group's new India campus is redesigning factories to fight back.
item.content:encoded: Extreme heat is hitting garment workers across Asia hard. See how Epic Group's new India campus is redesigning factories to fight back.

item.title: How A Palestinian Town Is Defending Itself Against Israeli Attacks

media:content.media:description: "We have nobody. So we are forced to stay here and protect this town."

item.description: "We have been left on our own. You are facing settlers supported by their government," said Fadi Alwan, one of the volunteers.
item.content:encoded: "We have been left on our own. You are facing settlers supported by their government," said Fadi Alwan, one of the volunteers.

item.title: Trump Ditches Qatari-Gifted Jet For Air Force One For "Old Time's Sake"

media:content.media:description: Trump offered little clarity on the swap.

item.description: The travel switch raised fresh security questions about the new aircraft that the US spent $400 million to retrofit.
item.content:encoded: The travel switch raised fresh security questions about the new aircraft that the US spent $400 million to retrofit.

item.title: How Quran Verses At Ali Khamenei's Funeral Became Iran's Diplomatic Language

media:content.media:description: A simple religious ceremony has turned into a heated debate in Iran.

item.description: The most heated debate involved the Saudi Arabian delegation headed by Deputy Foreign Minister Waleed bin Abdulkarim.
item.content:encoded: The most heated debate involved the Saudi Arabian delegation headed by Deputy Foreign Minister Waleed bin Abdulkarim.

item.title: "Thought I Was Gonna Die": US Teen Survives Freak Lightning Strike While Gaming In Bedroom

media:content.media:description: The family believes lightning first struck a nearby tree before the electricity entered their home

item.description: According to the teen, the electrical current reached him through a metal part of his desk that was touching his abdomen.
item.content:encoded: According to the teen, the electrical current reached him through a metal part of his desk that was touching his abdomen.

item.title: Chaos At Ali Khamenei's Iraq Funeral As US And Iran Exchange Strikes

media:content.media:description: Mourners storm Khamenei funeral in Iraq as Gulf war strikes intensify

item.description: Thousands mobbed Khamenei's casket at chaotic Iraq funerals as fresh U.S. and Iranian strikes threatened to shatter an interim peace deal.
item.content:encoded: Thousands mobbed Khamenei's casket at chaotic Iraq funerals as fresh U.S. and Iranian strikes threatened to shatter an interim peace deal.

item.title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Sirens Activated In Bahrain's Manama As Iran Attacks US Assets

media:content.media:description: The US also revoked a sanctions waiver for Iranian oil.

item.description: Iran-US War News LIVE: The US military has hit a variety of military sites and port facilities after blaming Iran for targeting several merchant vessels off the coast of Oman, sparking Iranian fire then as well.
item.content:encoded: Iran-US War News LIVE: The US military has hit a variety of military sites and port facilities after blaming Iran for targeting several merchant vessels off the coast of Oman, sparking Iranian fire then as well.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | After US-Iran, Why Is Pakistan Suddenly 'Brokering' Peace In Libya Now? Hint: $4 Billion

media:content.media:description: Pakistan Army Chief Asim Munir in Benghazi, Libya, in December 2025

item.description: According to reports, Pakistan became one of the interlocutors alongside the US, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Turkey.
item.content:encoded: According to reports, Pakistan became one of the interlocutors alongside the US, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Turkey.

item.title: US Officer Shoots Mexico Man With No Criminal Conviction, Family Seeks Probe

media:content.media:description: The shooting happened on Tuesday in Magnolia Park in Houston.

item.description: The shooting was at least the eighth death resulting from an encounter with federal immigration officers since the start of the immigration crackdown.
item.content:encoded: The shooting was at least the eighth death resulting from an encounter with federal immigration officers since the start of the immigration crackdown.

item.title: Inside Sri Lanka's Spy-Thriller Probe Into Deadly 2019 'Easter Bombings'

media:content.media:description: Sri Lanka says seven Sunni Muslim suicide bombers attacked three luxury hotels and three churches

item.description: The so-called Easter bombings on April 21, 2019, which killed 279 people and wounded more than 500, were blamed on Islamist militants.
item.content:encoded: The so-called Easter bombings on April 21, 2019, which killed 279 people and wounded more than 500, were blamed on Islamist militants.

item.title: Instructor Opens Aircraft Door, Jumps Mid-Flight. Stuuned Student Forced To Land Plane

media:content.media:description: Despite the shock, Rosario managed to remain calm and safely landed the aircraft without damage.

item.description: Leandro Andrés Bertazzo, 42, unbuckled his seatbelt, removed his headset, opened the aircraft door and jumped from the plane.
item.content:encoded: Leandro Andrés Bertazzo, 42, unbuckled his seatbelt, removed his headset, opened the aircraft door and jumped from the plane.

item.title: "US Bases Our Problem, Not Neighbours": Iranian Experts On Middle East Attacks

media:content.media:description: Iranian experts said that Khamenei's death in the US-Israel strikes has "united the people of Iran".

item.description: Prof Seyed Emamain, Founder of Governance and Policy Thinktank (GPTT), and Ali Salehian, Head of the International Department GPTT, have spoken to NDTV about the mass uprising against Khamenei's rule in January to mass support after his death.
item.content:encoded: Prof Seyed Emamain, Founder of Governance and Policy Thinktank (GPTT), and Ali Salehian, Head of the International Department GPTT, have spoken to NDTV about the mass uprising against Khamenei's rule in January to mass support after his death.
